channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Floridians cleaning up from Hurricane Milton are hampered by a widespread fuel shortage
item.description: CORTEZ, Fla. (AP) — Floridians recovering from Hurricane Milton, many of whom were journeying home after fleeing hundreds of miles to escape the storm, spent much of Saturday searching for gas as a fuel shortage gripped the state. In St.
item.dc:creator: RUSS BYNUM, BRENDAN FARRINGTON and TY ONEIL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Inspectors find bed bugs at Bellagio, Cosmopolitan hotels in Las Vegas
item.description: A report reveals health inspectors found bed bugs at some prominent hotels on the Las Vegas Strip during a six-month period.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Peguero, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How schools are tackling misinformation as false claims about hurricanes, election run wild
item.description: Misinformation generally has been an increasing concern, most recently with how the Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA) has been responding to the aftermath of Hurricanes Helene and Milton.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KREX 5

item.title: Heavy Israeli bombardment in northern Gaza as UN peacekeepers in Lebanon are hit again
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Palestinians in northern Gaza described heavy Israeli bombardment Saturday in the hours after airstrikes killed at least 22 people, as Israel warned people there and in southern Lebanon to get out of the way of offensives against the Hamas and Hezbollah militant groups.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Doorbell video shows murder suspects posing as energy workers before allegedly killing jewelry store owner
item.description: Chilling footage has been released showing two fake energy workers calling a house in Detroit before they allegedly murdered a 72-year-old jewelry store owner in the basement while his wife was duct-taped.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: With new rule changes, are real estate agents still necessary?
item.description: Some are deciding to sell their real estate without an agent and finding the process easier than expected.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, KRON4

item.title: Kentucky woman arrested after dismembered body parts found 'cooked' inside oven
item.description: A witness told state police that she was reportedly "casting spells" and being "confrontational" the day prior to the discovery.
item.dc:creator: Jace O'Barto, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Who is not allowed to vote in the 2024 presidential election?
item.description: The United States is just weeks away from a highly-anticipated presidential election, but not everyone will be able to exercise their right to vote on Nov. 5.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, NewsNation

item.title: Fifth Circuit weighs fate of DACA in case likely headed to Supreme Court
item.description: Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals has survived a litany of court challenges, but it is on the ropes amid a Texas-led lawsuit that challenges its legality.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, NewsNation

item.title: Missing Texas mother: Suzanne Simpson's neighbor witnessed fight
item.description: Simpson, a mother of four and real estate agent, disappeared after an alleged fight with her husband Brad Simpson, 53.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Residents slog through flooded streets, clear debris after Hurricane Milton tore through Florida
item.description: LITHIA, Fla. (AP) — Florida residents slogged through flooded streets, gathered up scattered debris and assessed damage to their homes on Friday after Hurricane Milton smashed through coastal communities and spawned a barrage of deadly tornadoes.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS O'MEARA, BRENDAN FARRINGTON and TY O'NEIL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 3 Europeans engineered $95M Texas Lotto win with over 25M tickets
item.description: It was successful for one group last year, but Texas Lottery officials have made changes to inhibit anyone else from trying it.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Elevator door was 'broken' during Colorado mine tour accident that killed 1 and trapped 12
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Investigators on Friday were trying to figure out what led to an elevator accident inside a former Colorado gold mine that killed a tour guide, injured four others and left a separate group of 12 people trapped for hours at the bottom of the tourist attraction 1,000 feet (305 meters) b
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN, AMY BETH HANSON, MEAD GRUVER and JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Which costume ideas are trending in your area? Google's Frightgeist has an idea
item.description: The search interest in specific costumes appeared to vary widely from region to region, but there were definitely some common themes.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: This restaurant-based Halloween costume is trending in Las Vegas, according to Google
item.description: The search interest in specific costumes appeared to vary widely from region to region, but there were definitely some common themes.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Experts warn 'crazy busy' Atlantic hurricane season is far from over
item.description: Millions of people in the southeastern U.S. still are reeling from the catastrophic damage caused by Hurricanes Helene and Milton, but scientists warn that the Atlantic hurricane season is far from over. “As far as hurricane landfalls in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ISABELLA O'MALLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Satellite images show before and after Hurricane Milton along Gulf Coast
item.description: Satellite images showcases the widespread damage and destruction left behind in Florida after Hurricane Milton.
item.dc:creator: Juan Cisneros, NewsNation

item.title: 1 killed, 23 rescued after equipment malfunction at tourist gold mine in Colorado
item.description: One person was killed and 23 were rescued after being trapped for about six hours inside a former Colorado gold mine when an elevator malfunctioned at the tourist site, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, ABC 4

item.title: Massive hole swallows 2 homes in Florida
item.description: The wild scene raised concerns about whether the ground collapse was caused by a sinkhole or water damage from sewage issues.
item.dc:creator: Austin Kellerman, ABC 4

item.title: Residents slog through flooded streets, pick up debris after Milton
item.description: Florida residents slogged through flooded streets, gathered up scattered debris and assessed damage to their homes on Friday after Hurricane Milton smashed through coastal communities and tore homes to pieces, flooded streets and spawned a barrage of deadly tornadoes.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 1 person dies and 12 are rescued after elevator malfunctions at Colorado gold mine tourist site
item.description: DENVER (AP) — One person was killed and 12 people were rescued after being trapped for about six hours at the bottom of a former Colorado gold mine when an elevator malfunctioned at the tourist site, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN and MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: A federal judge rejects a call to reopen voter registration in Georgia after Hurricane Helene
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A federal judge said Thursday that she won't order the presidential battleground state of Georgia to reopen voter registration for November’s elections despite recent disruptions to registration caused by Hurricane Helene. U.S.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Sean 'Diddy' Combs' sex trafficking trial set for May
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A May 5 trial date was set Thursday in Sean “Diddy” Combs'sex trafficking case, and a prosecutor argued that the jailed hip-hop mogul's lawyers were trying to exclude a “damning piece of evidence” by claiming it was leaked by the government. Assistant U.S.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: 14-year-old found floating on fence in Tampa floodwaters after Hurricane Milton
item.description: A 14-year-old had to be rescued after Hurricane Milton's floodwaters trapped him on a floating fence in the middle of Tampa.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, KRON4

item.title: CNN's Anderson Cooper hit by flying object during Milton live shot
item.description: CNN’s Anderson Cooper was hit by a flying object on Wednesday during a live shot while reporting from Florida on Hurricane Milton.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Trump's 'God Bless the USA' Bibles printed in China, records show
item.description: Thousands of copies of former President Trump's "God Bless the USA" Bible were printed in China, according to trade records.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, WGN 9

item.title: Lighting and furniture store to pay $4.1M in 'deceptive' pricing lawsuit
item.description: A lighting and furniture company based in California will pay more than $4 million to settle a civil lawsuit that accused the corporation of deceptive pricing and false advertising. Lamps Plus, Inc.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, Fox 5

item.title: 20 lbs. of cocaine found in truck floorboard at South Texas port of entry
item.description: A truck driven by a U.S. citizen had over 20 pounds of cocaine hidden in the floorboards as it tried to enter the South Texas border from Mexico, U.S. Customs and Border Protection officials said.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, WGN 9

item.title: Polluted waste from Florida's fertilizer industry is in the path of Milton's fury
item.description: As Hurricane Milton pummeled Florida’s west coast with powerful winds and flooding rain, environmentalists worry it could scatter the polluted leftovers of the state’s phosphate fertilizer mining industry and other hazardous waste across the peninsula and into vulnerable waterways.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL BIESECKER and JASON DEAREN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump-Putin ties are back in the spotlight after new book describes calls
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A new book's assertion that former President Donald Trump may have had as many as seven private phone calls with Russian President Vladimir Putin since leaving the White House has refocused attention on their politically fraught relationship and on Trump's sustained dialogue with w
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: State police recruit's death in Massachusetts overshadows graduation ceremony
item.description: WORCESTER, Mass. (AP) — Holding signs demanding truth and chanting for justice, several dozen people gathered Wednesday outside a graduation ceremony for Massachusetts State Police cadets demanding an explanation of how one of the recruits died during a training exercise.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Landfall timeline: When will Hurricane Milton hit Florida?
item.description: Hurricane Milton continues to push closer to the Florida coastline, and the impacts begin well before landfall.
item.dc:creator: Leigh Spann, Fox 5

item.title: NC National Guard helicopter crew grounded after 'rotor wash' incident blows away Helene aid
item.description: Video of the incident appears to show the wind from the helicopter blowing over tents and scattering items in the air.
item.dc:creator: Rodney Overton, Fox 5

item.title: Plane makes emergency landing in New York City after pilot dies mid-flight
item.description: Crew members on the Turkish Airlines flight worked to revive the pilot, but he died before the plane landed.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Biden postpones trip to Germany and Angola to monitor Hurricane Milton
item.description: President Joe Biden said Tuesday he is postponing a planned trip to Germany and Angola to stay at the White House to monitor Hurricane Milton, as it spins closer to Florida’s Gulf Coast.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The Latest: Hurricane Milton takes aim near Tampa Bay, Florida, once again a Category 5 storm
item.description: Hurricane Milton remains a ferocious storm that could land a once-in-a-century direct hit on Tampa and St. Petersburg, engulfing the populous region with towering storm surges and turning debris from Helene’s devastation 12 days ago into projectiles. While the storm had previously weakened, the U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Beware of Hurricane Helene relief scams, officials warn
item.description: In the wake of Hurricane Helene, thousands of people have responded to assist with recovery efforts — but officials are now warning of hurricane-related fraud schemes.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4
